description:
You are working as an automation specialist who is documenting the exact version of critical tools on a freshly provisioned Ubuntu-like CI runner.

Objective  
1. Determine the currently installed version of the package named “curl”.  
2. Write that single version string into a brand-new text file at  
   /home/user/curl_version.log

Output requirements for /home/user/curl_version.log  
• The file must contain exactly one line.  
• The line must follow this precise key–value pattern (no spaces before or after the “=” sign):  
  version=<VERSION_STRING>  
  Example of a correct line (do NOT hard-code this value):  
  version=7.88.1-10ubuntu1  
• No additional text, blank lines, comments, or trailing whitespace are allowed.

Additional constraints  
• Accomplish the entire task with a single terminal command (a pipeline is fine; it will still count as one compound command).  
• Do not edit the file manually; generate it directly from the command’s output redirection.  
• Assume you have normal (non-root) permissions and that the package database is already up to date.

The automated test suite will:  
1. Read /home/user/curl_version.log  
2. Verify that the file exists, is readable, contains exactly one line, and that the line matches the pattern above.  
3. Run “dpkg -s curl” internally to confirm the version string you captured is accurate.

Make sure the file path and format are strictly followed; any deviation will cause the test to fail.
truth:
Expected system state before the agent starts  
• Package “curl” is installed and registered in dpkg.  
• Running the command “dpkg-query -W -f='${Version}\n' curl” on the host returns:  
  7.74.0-1ubuntu2.18

Verification data  
• After successful completion, the file /home/user/curl_version.log must exist with 1 line:  
  version=7.74.0-1ubuntu2.18
